sourceUrl: Pre-existing in repo root as 'Sample thesis for testing.pdf' (real institutional master's thesis, Aalto University ICT Innovation programme). Copied into corpus.
groundTruthFacts: Title: 'Towards Connected Intelligence: Empowering Robotic Applications with Agentic AI Frameworks'
Author is Alberto Dian; supervisor Prof. Ville Kyrki; dated 21 June 2025; collaborative partner Huawei Munich Research Center
The proposed system is called AgenticVLA, deployed on the Mercury X1 humanoid robot using a Meta Quest 3 headset for VR teleoperation
The success rate of AgenticVLA was 44%, while the baseline models were not able to complete the task
OpenVLA is a 7B-parameter open-source VLA finetuned on 970k robot manipulation trajectories; OpenVLA-OFT achieves 43x faster throughput than base OpenVLA
Keywords: Embodied AI, Robotics, VLAs, AI Agents, 6G
notes: PROVENANCE: real 66-page master's thesis already present in the repo (git status shows it as the untracked/large fixture 'Sample thesis for testing.pdf', 25MB, PDF v1.5). Renamed to institutional_thesis.pdf for clarity. This is the PRIMARY thesis for academic-defense questions. Rich source of exact figures (44% SR, 7B params, 43x throughput).

sourceUrl: Pre-existing repo fixture: tests/fixtures/modes/custom/seminar-presentation/seminar_real_thesis.pdf. Copied into corpus.
groundTruthFacts: Despite the filename 'thesis', the actual content is a Data Analyst Job Description (2 pages)
Lists ETL responsibilities: Extract, transform, and load (ETL) data from various sources
Requires a Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, or related field; Master's preferred
Names specific tools: SQL, Python, R for analysis; Tableau, Power BI for visualization
notes: PROVENANCE NOTE / GOTCHA: this repo fixture is NAMED 'seminar_real_thesis.pdf' but its real content is a Data Analyst JD placeholder (confirmed by extraction via pdf-parse). Documented in project MEMORY. Retained because (a) it is a genuine repo artifact and (b) it is a useful negative/mismatch case: questions treating it as a thesis should NOT find thesis content. Used mainly for Data-Analyst mode role-context, not academic-defense facts.

groundTruthFacts: Proposes the Transformer architecture, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions
Achieves 28.4 BLEU on WMT 2014 English-to-German and 41.8 BLEU on WMT 2014 English-to-French (Transformer big)
Model dimension dmodel = 512; the big model was trained for 3.5 days on 8 NVIDIA P100 GPUs
Uses the Adam optimizer with beta1=0.9, beta2=0.98, epsilon=1e-9, and warmup_steps = 4000
Encoder and decoder each have 6 layers (N=6); multi-head attention uses dk = dv = dmodel/h = 64
notes: Real arXiv download (2.1MB, PDF v1.5, 15 pages). Chosen for its tables (Table 2 BLEU/FLOPs, Table 3 ablations) and equations (positional encoding, scaled dot-product attention) to stress the parser. Authors: Vaswani et al., Google Brain/Research.

groundTruthFacts: BERT = Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers; pre-trained with a Masked Language Model (MLM) and Next Sentence Prediction (NSP)
Pushes the GLUE score to 80.5% (7.7 point absolute improvement)
BERTBASE: L=12, H=768, A=12, Total Parameters=110M. BERTLARGE: L=24, H=1024, A=16, Total Parameters=340M
Raises SQuAD v1.1 Test F1 to 93.2 (1.5 point absolute improvement) and MultiNLI accuracy to 86.7%
The MLM randomly masks tokens (15%) and predicts the original vocabulary id of the masked word
notes: Real arXiv download (760KB, PDF v1.5). Chosen for parameter-count tables and GLUE/SQuAD result tables. Authors: Devlin, Chang, Lee, Toutanova (Google AI Language).

groundTruthFacts: Title: 'Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition'; introduces residual learning with reference to layer inputs (identity shortcut connections)
Evaluates residual nets with a depth of up to 152 layers on ImageNet — 8x deeper than VGG nets but still lower complexity
An ensemble of these residual nets achieves 3.57% error on the ImageNet test set
Won 1st place on the ILSVRC 2015 classification task, and 1st on ImageNet detection, ImageNet localization, COCO detection, and COCO segmentation
Identifies the 'degradation' problem: accuracy saturates then degrades with increased depth, not caused by overfitting
notes: Real arXiv download (804KB, PDF v1.5). Chosen as the systems/vision paper with result tables and the residual-block equation. Authors: He, Zhang, Ren, Sun (Microsoft Research).

groundTruthFacts: 142 data rows; columns: country, pop, continent, lifeExp, gdpPercap
United States 2007: pop 301139947, continent Americas, lifeExp 78.242, gdpPercap 42951.65309
China has the largest population at 1318683096; Japan has the highest life expectancy at 82.603
Norway has the highest GDP per capita at 49357.19017; Swaziland has the lowest life expectancy at 39.613
Continent row counts: Africa 52, Asia 33, Europe 30, Americas 25, Oceania 2
notes: Real public dataset (Gapminder 2007 snapshot). This is the PRIMARY spreadsheet substitute (app has NO xlsx support; CSV is the real-data spreadsheet analog). Small (8KB) with clean numeric columns ideal for Data-Analyst aggregation questions with exact answer keys.

groundTruthFacts: 13978 data rows; columns: Country Name, Country Code, Year, Value (GDP in current US$)
United States GDP in 2016 = 18804913000000.0 (~18.8 trillion USD)
China GDP in 2016 = 11233313730348.719 (~11.2 trillion USD)
Year range spans 1960 to 2023; some country names are quoted because they contain commas (e.g. 'Bahamas, The')
notes: Real World Bank GDP dataset (via datasets/gdp GitHub mirror, 552KB). SECONDARY dataset used for cross-document questions (pairs with gapminder for GDP comparisons) and large-CSV parser stress. Note quoted comma-containing country names is a CSV-parsing edge case.

groundTruthFacts: Stanford CS231n Lecture 1: Introduction, dated 4/4/2017, by Fei-Fei Li, Justin Johnson & Serena Yeung
48 slides covering the history of computer vision and the ImageNet/ILSVRC challenge
References Hubel & Wiesel 1959 receptive-field experiments and Larry Roberts' 'Block World' (1963)
Cites the 2012 AlexNet breakthrough (Krizhevsky, Sutskever, Hinton, NIPS 2012) and LeNet (1998)
Mentions the Cambrian explosion (~543 million years B.C.) as motivation for the evolution of vision
notes: Real Stanford lecture slide deck (16MB PDF, 48 slides). This is the SLIDE-DECK CONTENT deliverable — app has NO pptx ingest, so real slide content is supplied as PDF (the format the parser accepts). Used for Investor-Pitch / Consulting slide-reading and Conference-Talk mode questions.

groundTruthFacts: RFC 8259: 'The JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) Data Interchange Format', by T. Bray, Ed. (Textuality), December 2017
It Obsoletes RFC 7159 and is Standards Track (STD 90), ISSN 2070-1721
JSON text exchanged between systems that are not part of a closed ecosystem MUST be encoded using UTF-8
Defines six structural characters and is also described by ECMA-404 (a normative reference)
899 lines of plain text; acknowledges Douglas Crockford as originator of JSON
notes: Real IETF RFC as plain text (28KB). The plain-text / structured-standard document. Used for Legal/Compliance-style 'what does the spec mandate' questions with exact normative-language answer keys (MUST/UTF-8).

groundTruthFacts: Title/opening: 'Demonstration of DOCX support in calibre'
States it demonstrates the calibre DOCX Input plugin converting typographic features in a Microsoft Word (2007 and newer) document
Lists supported features: images, tables, lists, footnotes, endnotes, links, dropcaps, and text/paragraph-level formatting
Mentions converting to modern ebook formats such as AZW3 for Kindles or EPUB for other ebook readers
notes: SUBSTITUTION LOGGED: the first two candidate DOCX URLs (microsoft/presidio raw path, python-docx test files) returned 404/HTML. Fell back to the calibre demo.docx (a real, valid Microsoft Word 2007+ file, PK/OOXML magic verified, 1.3MB). This is a genuine .docx to exercise the mammoth parser path.

groundTruthFacts: 6-page PDF whose pages are embedded raster images with essentially NO extractable text layer
pdf-parse extracts only ~96 characters total across all 6 pages (just page-break artifacts)
Content is a 'smile' image rendered via multiple ImageMagick encodings (ASCII85, LZW, CCITTFax)
notes: The DELIBERATELY NASTY case: a genuine image-only (scanned-analog) PDF. Any question over this doc MUST refuse or state no text is extractable — it is the robustness / no-usable-content stressor for the ingest pipeline. SUBSTITUTION NOTE: an earlier pdf995 sample turned out to have normal text (not nasty) and was discarded in favor of this true image-only PDF.

groundTruthFacts: Single-page PDF containing the Arabic word 'habibi' with right-to-left / CID font encoding
pdf-parse extracts only ~43 characters, and they are garbled/reordered ('habibi' plus mangled Arabic glyphs)
Demonstrates unusual-encoding extraction where the visible text does not map cleanly to Unicode
notes: Second NASTY case: unusual RTL/CID text encoding. Stresses the parser's Unicode handling; correct behavior is to avoid asserting confident facts from the garbled extraction.

groundTruthFacts: Valid Microsoft OOXML PowerPoint file (PK/ZIP magic verified, 44KB)
The app's server-side allow-list (electron/ipcHandlers.ts:7758-7770) does NOT include .pptx
Uploading this file MUST be rejected by the upload handler (unsupported extension), not parsed
notes: REJECTION-PATH fixture only. Real .pptx obtained from pandoc's test corpus after two other candidate URLs 404'd; found via the GitHub contents API. Purpose is to verify the allow-list rejects unsupported OOXML presentations. It is NOT part of the answerable knowledge corpus.
